KAUA‘I COUNTY COUNCIL

MEETING NOTICE AND AGENDA

WEDNESDAY, MAY 13, 2026

8:30 AM OR SOON THEREAFTER

COUNCIL CHAMBERS, HISTORIC COUNTY BUILDING

4396 RICE STREET, SUITE 201

LīHUʻE KAUAʻI, HAWAIʻI 96766

AND

MEETING ROOM 106, HISTORIC COUNTY BUILDING

4396 RICE STREET, FIRST FLOOR, LīHUʻE KAUAʻI, HAWAIʻI 96766

MEETING INFORMATION:

This is an in-person meeting at multiple meeting sites connected by interactive conference

technology pursuant to Hawai‘i Revised Statutes (HRS) Section 92-3.5. Councilmembers will

attend the meeting at the Council Chambers location only. Members of the public may attend the

meeting at either location.

Oral Testimony: Accepted in-person at the Council Chambers and Meeting Room 106.

Written Testimony: May be submitted for any meeting via: email to

counciltestimony@kauai.gov; fax to (808) 241-6349; mail to the Office of the County Clerk,

Council Services Division at 4396 Rice Street, Suite 209, Lihu‘e, Hawai‘i, 96766; or hand- delivery at Suite 209 and Meeting Room 106 during regular business hours (7:45 a.m. to 4:30

p.m., Monday through Friday, excluding holidays).

Courtesy Livestream, Video, and Minutes: Meeting livestream (recorded video to follow) is

available at www.kauai.gov/Government/Council/Webcast-Meetings. If the courtesy livestream

is unavailable for any reason, the meeting will proceed. Meeting minutes will be completed

pursuant to HRS Chapter 92 and electronically posted at www.kauai.gov/Government/Council

following Council approval.

Courtesy Remote Oral Testimony: This is not a remote meeting pursuant to HRS Section 92-3.7.

The option of remote oral testimony is offered as a courtesy only. To register, testifiers shall sign

up via emailing remote.signup@kauai.gov, indicating the agenda item(s) they would like to

provide testimony on. Emails are due no later than 12:00 Noon the business day prior to the

meeting. Those who timely register will receive remote testimony access and instructions via

reply email. Those wishing to view the meeting but not provide testimony will be directed to the

courtesy livestream (via the website link above). If the courtesy remote testimony access is

unavailable for any reason, the meeting will proceed.

Additional Note for Both Methods of Oral Testimony: Generally, each agenda item is addressed

(and oral testimony received) in the order listed in the agenda; however, agenda items may occur

out of order without notice and oral testimony is not accepted for completed agenda items. Those

seeking the guaranteed ability to testify should report: (1) in-person, and (2) by the meeting start

time.

For Further Assistance: If you need an auxiliary aid/service, other accommodation due to a

disability, or an interpreter for non-English speaking persons, please contact the Office of the

County Clerk, Council Services Division at (808) 241-4188 or cokcouncil@kauai.gov as soon as

possible. Requests made as early as possible will allow adequate time to fulfill your request.

Upon request, this notice is available in alternate formats such as large print, braille, or electronic

COUNTY COUNCIL MEETING AGENDA MAY 13, 2026

1

Page 2 of 6

A. MEETING CALLED TO ORDER.

B. ROLL CALL.

C. APPROVAL OF AGENDA.

D. MINUTES of the following meetings of the Council:

1. March 25, 2026 Council Meeting

2. April 8, 2026 Council Meeting

3. April 8, 2026 Public Hearing re: Bill No. 2987

4. April 22, 2026 Public Hearing re: Bill No. 2990 and Bill No. 2991

E. CONSENT CALENDAR:

1. C 2026-103 Communication (03/12/2026) from the Mayor, transmitting for Council

consideration and confirmation, the appointment of Leah L. Ragsac to the Board of Review –

Partial term ending 12/31/2026. (See Resolution No. 2026-15)

2. C 2026-104 Communication (04/14/2026) from the Director of Finance, transmitting for

Council information, the Third Quarter Statement of Equipment Purchases for Fiscal Year

2025-2026, pursuant to Section 22 of Ordinance No. B-2025-905, as amended, relating to the

Operating Budget of the County of Kaua‘i for Fiscal Year 2025-2026.

3. C 2026-105 Communication (04/28/2026) from Council Chair Rapozo and Council Vice

Chair Kuali‘i, transmitting for Council consideration, a Resolution Authorizing The

Acquisition Of A Public Pedestrian Beach Access Easement In Land Required For Public

Use To Wit: A Public Pedestrian Beach Access To ‘Aliomanu Bay, Anahola, County Of

Kaua‘i, Hawai‘i, And Determining And Declaring The Necessity Of The Acquisition Thereof

By Eminent Domain (For Condemnation). (See Resolution No. 2026-16)

F. COMMUNICATIONS:

1. C 2026-106 Communication (03/12/2026) from the Executive on Transportation, requesting

copy.

ADDITIONAL NOTIFICATION – NO ELEVATOR ACCESS TO SECOND FLOOR:

At the time of this meeting notice and agenda posting, the Historic County Building elevator is

temporarily inoperable. Because the Council Chambers is on the second floor of the building, an in- person meeting site at Meeting Room 106 will be connected to the in-person meeting site at the Council

Chambers by interactive conference technology. If audio communication to the meeting cannot be

maintained between the locations, the meeting shall be automatically recessed to restore

communication. If the audio communication cannot be restored within thirty (30) minutes, the meeting

will be terminated. If the audiovisual communication can be restored, the meeting may reconvene. If

the audio communication can be restored, the meeting may reconvene with audio-only communication,

with hardcopies of visual aids made available to all meeting participants pursuant to HRS Section 92-

3.5(c).

COUNTY COUNCIL MEETING AGENDA MAY 13, 2026

2

Page 3 of 6

Council approval to apply for, receive, expend, and indemnify the Federal Transit

Administration (FTA) for FTA Section 5339 formula grant in the amount of $3,629,152.00

for nine (9) diesel gas buses, and FTA Section 5311 formula grant in the amount of

$1,460,150.00 for three (3) diesel gas buses, which are soon to be beyond their useful lives,

to be used to fund eighty percent (80%) of the purchase cost, and twenty percent (20%) from

the Transportation Agency’s Fiscal Year 2027 budget request.

2. C 2026-107 Communication (04/11/2026) from Darren D. Rose, Assistant Chief of Police,

requesting Council approval of the indemnification clause included in the Smiths Detection

US Terms and Conditions of Service, ensuring that the Holding Facility’s X-ray screening

equipment remains fully operational, properly calibrated, and compliant with all applicable

safety and security standards. Maintaining this coverage allows the Kaua‘i Police Department

to uphold reliable screening capabilities, minimize equipment downtime, and support

uninterrupted security operations.

3. C 2026-108 Communication (04/13/2026) from the Director of Finance, requesting Council

approval to accept the terms contained in the First Hawaiian Bank eDeposit Agreement,

which will allow the County the ability to scan eligible checks and transmit electronically to

First Hawaiian Bank for deposit to the County’s bank accounts.

4. C 2026-109 Communication (04/14/2026) from the Executive on Aging, requesting Council

approval to receive and expend State funds in the amount of $55,280.00, and to indemnify

the State Executive Office on Aging, for the provision of Aging and Disability Resource

Center (ADRC) Services, subject to the allotments to be made by the Director of Finance,

State of Hawai‘i pursuant to Chapter 37, Hawai‘i Revised Statutes, for the period July 1, 2025

through June 30, 2026.

5. C 2026-110 Communication (04/14/2026) from the Executive on Aging, requesting Council

approval to receive and expend State funds in the amount of $50,000.00, and to indemnify

the State Executive Office on Aging, for the Healthy Aging Partnership (HAP), which will be

used towards Better Choices Better Health (BCBH) and Enhance Fitness (EF), proven

effective through evaluation, demonstrating their ability to improve the health and well-being

of older adults or reduce disease, disability, and/or injury, subject to the allotments to be

made by the Director of Finance, State of Hawai‘i pursuant to Chapter 37, Hawai‘i Revised

Statutes, for the period July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026.

6. C 2026-111 Communication (04/20/2026) from Makana A. Rivera, Assistant Chief of Police,

requesting Council approval to purchase from the Account No. 001-1003-551.24-00 in the

amount of $96,235.86, non-budgeted equipment of one (1) Spacefile Mobile Shelving

System, a high-density, track-mounted shelving unit that maximizes available space by

allowing shelving units to move, and to improve storage capacity and organization of

departmental records and equipment within the Kaua‘i Police Department’s (KPD’s)

Evidence Section.

7. C 2026-112 Communication (04/23/2026) from the Emergency Management Administrator,

requesting Council approval to receive and expend recurring Federal grant funds, in the

amount of $760,000.00, from the United States (U.S.) Department of Homeland Security, via

the State of Hawai‘i, Department of Law Enforcement, to continue to enhance the capability

of State and local units of government to prevent, deter, respond to, and recover from threats

and incidents of terrorism, as well as all hazards catastrophic preparedness initiatives. This
